title: Urinary tract infection in women - self-care
license_notes: Use as public patient-education source support. Avoid verbatim copying into primitives unless reuse terms are confirmed for the exact content.
supports: Patients treated for UTI should take antibiotics as prescribed and complete the course unless told otherwise.
Patients should drink fluids as allowed and avoid irritants if they worsen symptoms.
Return or urgent care is needed for fever, flank pain, vomiting, worsening symptoms, or symptoms that do not improve.
